brainstorms.connection: A living surface visibly coated by dirty air feels immediate and real.

Polluted air often shows up as that fuzzy glow around city lights.
It taps into the most familiar everyday association people have with alcohol.
It points to alcohol's practical role outside drinks.
Many fragrances rely on alcohol as a fast-evaporating carrier.

It hints at cinnamaldehyde through a warm, familiar spice object.
Citrusy scent notes make aldehydes feel sensory instead of abstract.
Lithium makes the alkali metals feel modern and pocket-sized.
Sodium gives this whole metal family an everyday hook.
